metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns two PostgreSQL databases (primary + ToolJet DB), exposes CRUD operations on apps, users, organizations, data sources, workflows, and AI conversations, and manages entity lifecycle state. Multiple write endpoints exist alongside reads. Classified as stateful-crud.

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging configuration found in IaC
top_gaps.impact: No audit trail for forensic analysis or compliance

top_gaps.title: Auto-Scaling
top_gaps.gap: No auto-scaling configured; ECS service uses static desired_count=2
top_gaps.impact: Cannot respond to traffic spikes; over-provisioning during low demand

top_gaps.title: Backup and Recovery
top_gaps.gap: RDS has no backup_retention_period configured; defaults to 0
top_gaps.impact: Data loss risk with no recovery capability

top_gaps.title: SLO Definitions
top_gaps.gap: No SLO definitions found anywhere in the repository
top_gaps.impact: Cannot measure service quality or drive prioritization

top_gaps.title: Business Metrics
top_gaps.gap: Only infrastructure metrics (OpenTelemetry); no business outcome metrics
top_gaps.impact: No visibility into whether the system delivers business value

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 3). 29 GitHub Actions workflows.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers deployments, checks build status, manages releases across 29 workflows.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose GitHub Actions API access to the agent; create a workflow dispatch interface.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists in the repo (Docusaurus docs site).
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that indexes documentation and provides natural-language answers.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index the docs/ directory content and OpenAPI spec into a vector store.

quick_agent_wins.name: Data Query Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Database with clear schema (DATA-Q2 = 3). TypeORM with 80+ entity models.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Natural language to SQL agent for querying application data.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate schema documentation from TypeORM entities; implement query guardrails.

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Structured logging and tracing in place (OPS-Q1 = 3). Full OpenTelemetry.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that queries traces, correlates errors, and suggests root causes.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Ensure OTLP exporter targets a queryable backend; expose trace query API.

decomposition_strategy.rationale: NestJS module structure provides natural extraction boundaries. Team can sustain parallel development. Identifiable modules with shared DB coupling (APP-Q2=2).

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 (primary trigger: monolith with shared DB). Supporting: INF-Q1=3, APP-Q3=3, APP-Q4=3.

triggering_questions.note: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared database schemas

immediate_actions: Identify service boundaries from NestJS modules
Implement Anti-corruption Layer between monolith and new services

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=3 — compute already on ECS/Fargate and Kubernetes. 14 Dockerfiles and container definitions exist.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1=3 meets threshold; workload already containerized on ECS Fargate and Kubernetes

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload already runs on ECS Fargate and Kubernetes; container pathway does not apply.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=3 (minimal stored procedures). No commercial DB engines detected — PostgreSQL is already open source.

triggering_questions.note: DATA-Q4=3 meets threshold; no commercial database engines detected

pathways.not_triggered_reason: PostgreSQL is already open source. No Oracle, SQL Server, or other commercial database engines detected.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=2 (Redis self-managed as sidecar, RDS single-AZ without Multi-AZ failover).

triggering_questions.note: Redis self-managed as sidecar container; RDS single-AZ without Multi-AZ
Engine versions pinned but dev/prod mismatch

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads (ETL, streaming pipelines, analytics jobs) detected. BullMQ is a job queue, not analytics infrastructure.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q4=3 meets threshold; no analytics workloads detected

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing workloads exist. BullMQ handles job queuing, not data analytics or streaming.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=2 (partial IaC), OPS-Q5=2 (rolling only), OPS-Q6=2 (tests not consistently in CI).

triggering_questions.note: Partial IaC — operational resources not covered
Integration tests exist but not run consistently in CI

immediate_actions: Auto-trigger Cypress tests on every PR

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: AI/agent frameworks already present: AWS Bedrock SDK, OpenAI, Anthropic, vector DBs (Pinecone, Qdrant, Weaviate), internal AI module with agents.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: AI/agent frameworks already present in the codebase. AWS Bedrock, OpenAI, Anthropic SDKs, vector databases (Pinecone, Qdrant, Weaviate), and an internal AI module with agent patterns are all integrated.

findings.title: No CloudTrail Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail configuration found in any Terraform module. Application-level audit logs exist but no AWS infrastructure audit trail.
findings.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent infrastructure audit logging. AWS API actions are not captured. No immutable log storage.
findings.recommendation: Add aws_cloudtrail resource to Terraform with log file validation and S3 Object Lock for immutable storage. Enable for all management and data events on sensitive resources.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling Configured
findings.description: No auto-scaling configured. ECS service uses static desired_count=2. Kubernetes HPA disabled (min=max=1). No scaling policies on any resource.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned with no ability to scale up during traffic spikes or scale down during low demand.
findings.recommendation: Configure ECS Application Auto Scaling with target tracking. For EKS, configure HPA with custom metrics. Consider Karpenter for node scaling.

findings.title: No Backup and Recovery Configuration
findings.description: RDS has no backup_retention_period (defaults to 0). skip_final_snapshot=true. No AWS Backup plan. Redis data is ephemeral.
findings.gap: No backup configuration for any data store. RDS will not retain backups. No documented restore procedures.
findings.recommendation: Set backup_retention_period=7 on RDS with PITR. Remove skip_final_snapshot. Create AWS Backup plan. Migrate Redis to ElastiCache with backup.

findings.title: No SLO Definitions
findings.description: No SLO definitions found. No error budget tracking. Custom metrics exist but not tied to SLO targets.
findings.gap: No formal definition of acceptable service levels. Cannot measure service quality or drive prioritization.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for critical user journeys: app builder load time, data query success rate, workflow execution success rate, API availability.

findings.title: No Business Metrics
findings.description: Only infrastructure metrics (api.hits, api.duration, users.concurrent, sessions.active). No business outcome metrics.
findings.gap: No visibility into whether the system delivers business value. No metrics for apps created, queries executed, or workflow completions.
findings.recommendation: Add business outcome metrics: apps_created_total, data_queries_success/failure, workflows_completed/failed, user_onboarding_completed.

findings.title: No Anomaly Detection or Alerting
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms in Terraform. No alerting configuration beyond Sentry error reporting. No anomaly detection.
findings.gap: No proactive alerting on error rates, latency, or resource utilization. Sentry provides reactive error reporting only.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for API error rate, p99 latency, ECS task failures, RDS connections. Enable anomaly detection on critical paths.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, SSM Automation documents, or self-healing automation. Incident response is entirely ad hoc.
findings.gap: No documented procedures for common failure scenarios. No automated remediation.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common incidents. Implement as SSM Automation documents. Consider self-healing patterns for auto-restart and circuit breakers.

findings.title: Self-Managed Redis and Single-AZ RDS
findings.description: RDS managed but single-AZ without Multi-AZ failover. Redis runs as self-managed sidecar container. Mixed managed/self-managed state.
findings.gap: Redis is self-managed. RDS lacks Multi-AZ failover. Production database infrastructure is partially managed.
findings.recommendation: Enable Multi-AZ on RDS. Migrate Redis to ElastiCache or MemoryDB with Multi-AZ. Consider Aurora PostgreSQL.

findings.title: Public Subnets with Missing Segmentation
findings.description: VPC configured with public subnets only (map_public_ip_on_launch=true). No private subnets, NAT gateway, or VPC endpoints.
findings.gap: Services deployed in public subnets with public IPs. No private subnet tier for application and database workloads.
findings.recommendation: Restructure VPC with private subnets for ECS tasks and RDS. Add NAT gateway and VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

findings.title: Database Single-AZ Deployment
findings.description: Compute spans 2 AZs but RDS is single-AZ. Redis as sidecar provides no independent HA. AZ failure would take down database.
findings.gap: Primary database is single-AZ. Single AZ failure would take down the database with no automatic recovery.
findings.recommendation: Enable multi_az=true on RDS. Migrate Redis to ElastiCache with Multi-AZ automatic failover.

findings.title: Partial IaC Coverage
findings.description: Terraform covers compute, VPC, ALB, RDS, IAM but NOT monitoring, alerting, backup, auto-scaling, Route 53, or WAF.
findings.gap: Operational resources (monitoring, alerting, backup, scaling) are missing from IaC. Significant manual infrastructure likely exists.
findings.recommendation: Expand Terraform to cover CloudWatch alarms, AWS Backup plans, auto-scaling policies, Route 53 health checks, and WAF rules.

findings.title: Monolith with Shared Database
findings.description: Single NestJS server containing 50+ modules with shared PostgreSQL database via TypeORM. Modules have identifiable boundaries but share schemas.
findings.gap: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared database schemas, direct cross-module entity access, and single deployment unit.
findings.recommendation: Begin Strangler Fig decomposition starting with loosely-coupled modules: Workflows, AI, ToolJet Database.

findings.title: API Versioning Not Actively Used
findings.description: NestJS URI versioning framework configured but default is VERSION_NEUTRAL. No @Version decorators or /v1/ patterns in use.
findings.gap: Versioning framework configured but not actively used. Breaking changes would affect all consumers simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Apply @Version decorators as APIs evolve. Version public API surface first (SCIM, external integrations).

findings.title: Unstructured Data Not in S3
findings.description: Files and assets stored on local filesystem or container volume. No S3 configuration for application storage.
findings.gap: Unstructured data in local/container filesystem rather than managed object storage. No parsing pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Migrate file storage to Amazon S3. Implement presigned URLs. Consider S3 lifecycle policies.

findings.title: No Infrastructure Encryption at Rest
findings.description: No KMS keys in Terraform. RDS has no explicit storage_encrypted. Application-level AES-256-GCM encryption exists but no infrastructure-level encryption.
findings.gap: Infrastructure-level encryption at rest (RDS storage, EBS) not explicitly configured. No customer-managed KMS keys.
findings.recommendation: Add storage_encrypted=true and customer-managed KMS key to RDS. Create KMS keys for all sensitive data stores.

findings.title: Credentials in Terraform Variable Defaults
findings.description: Terraform variables.tf has default values for sensitive vars (PG_PASS='postgres'). No rotation configured. Mix of Secrets Manager and env vars.
findings.gap: Hardcoded default credentials in Terraform variables. No automated rotation. Production may use env vars without rotation.
findings.recommendation: Remove default credential values from Terraform variables. Implement Secrets Manager rotation. Add sensitive=true to credential variables.

findings.title: No SAST in CI Pipeline
findings.description: Dependency scanning active (npm audit + Dependabot + Grype) but no SAST tool for code-level vulnerabilities. No container image scanning.
findings.gap: No SAST for application code. No container image scanning in Docker release pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add SAST scanning (CodeGuru Reviewer, Semgrep) to CI. Add container scanning to Docker release workflow.

findings.title: Rolling Deployments Only
findings.description: Kubernetes uses RollingUpdate with health checks. No canary, blue/green, or traffic-shifting configuration.
findings.gap: No progressive delivery. Bad deployments affect all users after rolling update completes.
findings.recommendation: Implement canary deployments with Argo Rollouts or AWS CodeDeploy blue/green. Add automated rollback on failure.

findings.title: Integration Tests Not Consistently in CI
findings.description: Cypress e2e tests exist but require manual label triggers. Not run automatically on every PR.
findings.gap: PRs can merge without integration test validation. Tests require explicit label triggering.
findings.recommendation: Auto-trigger Cypress tests on every PR. Run core smoke suite automatically; full suite on merge.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: CODEOWNERS covers only package.json and migrations. No per-service dashboards, no alarm ownership, no team attribution.
findings.gap: No observability ownership. Monitoring is reactive and fragmented. No dashboards or alarm ownership defined.
findings.recommendation: Extend CODEOWNERS for observability assets. Create per-module dashboards with named owners. Define on-call rotation.

findings.title: Inconsistent Resource Tagging
findings.description: Minimal tagging on resources. No default_tags in Terraform provider. No cost allocation, ownership, or environment tags.
findings.gap: No consistent tagging standard. No cost allocation tags. No tag enforcement.
findings.recommendation: Add default_tags to Terraform AWS provider. Implement required-tags Config rules. Activate cost allocation tags.

findings.title: Mix of Managed Compute with Sidecar Dependencies
findings.description: Primary workloads on ECS Fargate and Kubernetes. Redis runs as sidecar rather than managed service.
findings.gap: Redis bundled as sidecar container rather than using a managed service (ElastiCache/MemoryDB).
findings.recommendation: Migrate Redis from sidecar to Amazon ElastiCache. Standardize on EKS as primary orchestration platform.

findings.title: Partial Workflow Orchestration Adoption
findings.description: Temporal.io integrated for EE workflows. CE uses BullMQ job queues. Partial orchestration adoption.
findings.gap: Temporal is EE-only. CE relies on BullMQ (job queue) rather than dedicated workflow orchestration.
findings.recommendation: Consider AWS Step Functions as managed alternative to self-hosted Temporal. Evaluate for CE workflows.

findings.title: Messaging on Self-Managed Redis
findings.description: BullMQ provides functional async messaging but relies on self-managed Redis sidecar rather than AWS managed messaging.
findings.gap: Messaging infrastructure uses self-managed Redis. Event-driven patterns exist but backed by infrastructure requiring operational management.
findings.recommendation: Migrate Redis backing store to ElastiCache at minimum. Consider EventBridge for cross-service events as services are extracted.

findings.title: CI/CD Strong but Production Deployment Manual
findings.description: 29 workflows provide comprehensive CI/CD. Production deployment requires manual workflow_dispatch. No IaC apply pipeline.
findings.gap: Production deployment manual. No Terraform plan/apply pipeline. No automated rollback.
findings.recommendation: Add automated production deployment with approval gates. Implement Terraform plan/apply pipeline.

findings.title: ALB Without API Gateway Features
findings.description: ALB configured with health check but no API Gateway-level throttling, request validation, or edge auth.
findings.gap: No API Gateway features. Rate limiting handled at application level rather than edge.
findings.recommendation: Consider API Gateway in front of ALB for throttling and request validation. Implement WAF on ALB.
